marketState: cautiously bullish — extreme MM short positioning and imminent above-max heat demand surge offset near-term storage surplus and 1-7 day weather softness
summary: Front-month NG=F at $3.198 sits ~$0.24 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.958 but remains comfortably inside the $2.46–$3.46 band, with ~$0.26 headroom to the upper edge. The single most decisive signal is COT: managed-money net is -122,617 at the 3rd percentile of 5-year history — a historically extreme short position that creates acute short-squeeze risk once a demand catalyst fires. That catalyst is visibly building: while the next 7 days show demand running -20.9% below 5-year norms (mildly bearish near-term), the 8–16 day window is ABOVE THE HISTORICAL MAXIMUM (+51.3% vs 5y, rangePos 1.98), and current demand just surged +26.1 units in 24 hours to +20% above the 5-year normal. Storage remains a structural headwind at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +25 Bcf injection surprise, and seasonal reliability is LOW (44 pp 10-90 spread). Short-term technicals confirm the bifurcated picture: 5m/15m are oversold (RSI 32–38, price below lower Bollinger) while 1h/4h are constructively bullish (positive MACD, 50>200 EMA), though the daily and weekly 50<200 EMA cross warns the longer-term trend has not yet turned, giving the bull case a weather-dependent, catalyst-required character.
